text: The purpose of this study is to formulate the bases of quantum law as a law of the future through research of quantum phenomena and features of quantum technologies that may pose a threat, risks and challenges to the emergence of quantum technologies as well as the legal regulation of quantum technologies in Russia and abroad. For this purpose, the authors have applied a whole range of general scientific methods of the systemic approach as well as special methods of engineering and legal sciences. In particular, the comparative-legal method made it possible to characterise the main trends of the regulation of quantum technologies in the domestic and foreign legal frameworks by examining the international and national legislation in this field. The legal modelling method enabled us to formulate the concept of quantum law, and identify the main trends of its development and a set of ethical and legal principles. As a result, the study has identified the features of quantum technologies that could affect the development of law with the emergence of quantum technologies; the main risks and challenges related to quantum technologies; characterised the regulation of quantum technologies in a number of foreign countries and identified trends in regulating quantum technologies in Russia. Conclusion: the study formulated the conceptual bases of quantum law and outlined the vectors of its development. The study has scientific novelty and practical significance. The scientific novelty of the study is that, for the first time in the legal literature, a comprehensive analysis of current domestic (Russian and foreign) regulation of quantum technologies was carried out and an attempt was made to prove the need to establish quantum law and to identify its main trends of development. The practical significance of the study lies in that the conclusions and recommendations of the authors for improving the current regulation of quantum technologies may be used in the law-making and law-enforcement activities in this sphere, and in that they can lay the foundation for further scientific research in the sphere of quantum technologies.
